mnemonics: NEW Parent First (N-P-F): A New object calls its Parent's constructor First.
The Super Chain: To build an object, you must first build its superclass, link by link.
keyPoints: A constructor is called with the `new` keyword.
The first line of any constructor is an implicit call to `super()` (to the no-argument parent constructor), unless another constructor in the same class is explicitly called with `this()`.
Constructors execute from the top of the inheritance hierarchy down to the instantiated class.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that `super()` is implicitly called, leading to a compile-time error if the parent class lacks a no-argument constructor.
Placing code before an explicit `super()` or `this()` call, which is illegal in Java.
Assuming the order of initialization is the reverse (child first, then parent).
